name: Mola d'Ares Trail (SL-CV 46)
name_original: El Camí de la Mola de Ares (Circular SL-CV 46)
description: Hike the moderate 4.1-mile Mola d'Ares Trail (SL-CV 46) to explore unique "mola" landscapes, historic mills, and panoramic views.
region_intro: Hiking around Ares Del Maestrat offers diverse landscapes characterized by rugged mountains, deep ravines, and Mediterranean forests. The region is situated at an elevation of 1,148 meters, featuring prominent natural landmarks like the Mola d'Ares. Trails often traverse ancient bridle paths, showcasing historical dry stone constructions and unique geological formations. The area provides a variety of routes suitable for different skill levels. Best hiking trails around Ares Del Maestrat The most popular hiking route is Mola d'Ares Trail (SL-CV 46), a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) moderate trail that takes 1 hour 55 minutes to complete. This route explores the unique "mola" landscapes of the flat-topped Mola d'Ares. Another top favourite among local hikers is Hermitage of Sant Cristòfol – El Rivet Oak Grove loop from Benassal, a moderate 5.8 miles (9.3 km) path. This trail leads through Mediterranean forest featuring ancient oak trees. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Benassal, a 3.7 miles (6.0 km) easy trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 46 minutes. Hiking around Ares Del Maestrat is defined by spectacular flat-topped mountains, deep ravines, and ancient Mediterranean forests. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to more challenging trails. The routes in Ares Del Maestrat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Ares Del Maestrat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Ares Del Maestrat?
A: Ares Del Maestrat offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 40 trails to explore. These range from easy strolls to more challenging treks, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: What makes hiking in Ares Del Maestrat unique?
A: The region is characterized by its diverse landscapes, including spectacular flat-topped mountains like the Mola d'Ares, deep ravines, and ancient Mediterranean forests. Hikers can discover historical dry stone constructions, ancient bridle paths, and unique geological formations, all set against breathtaking panoramic views.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Ares Del Maestrat has 12 easy trails. A great option for a relaxed outing is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40342054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Benassal&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.7 miles (6.0 km) long and typically takes less than two hours to complete.

Q: Are there any circular walks in the area?
A: Many trails in Ares Del Maestrat are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A notable example is the historic Mill Route (La Ruta dels Molins), which follows old bridle paths connecting five flour mills and features unique elements like a 23-meter waterfall and an impressive aqueduct.

Q: Can I see historical sites or landmarks while hiking?
A: Absolutely. The trails often lead to or pass by significant historical sites. You can hike to the ruins of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1104473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ares Castle&lt;/a&gt; for spectacular views, or explore the charming &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1113753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Culla Historic Village&lt;/a&gt;. The region also boasts prehistoric cave paintings at Remigia Cave and Mola Remigia Cliff, part of a UNESCO World Heritage site.

Q: Are there any trails that feature waterfalls?
A: Yes, the historic Mill Route (La Ruta dels Molins) is known for its unique hydraulic system, which includes a 23-meter waterfall and a hexagonal waterfall, offering a fascinating natural and historical experience.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Hikers will encounter diverse natural beauty, including the iconic flat-topped &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1104476" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mola d'Ares&lt;/a&gt;, the Mediterranean forest of Barranc dels Horts with its ancient century oaks, and deep ravines that offer constantly changing scenery. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3004757" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rivet Municipal Nature Park&lt;/a&gt; is another highlight.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Ares Del Maestrat?
A: The trails in Ares Del Maestrat are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse landscapes, the well-preserved historical elements, and the breathtaking panoramic views from elevated points like the Mola d'Ares.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Ares Del Maestrat?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in Ares Del Maestrat. However, it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in nature reserves or areas with livestock, to ensure the safety of your pet and local wildlife.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Ares Del Maestrat?
A: The best times for hiking in Ares Del Maestrat are typically spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be hot, while winters can be chilly at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: While most trails are easy to moderate, there are options for more experienced hikers. The region's rugged terrain, with its significant elevation changes and deep ravines, provides opportunities for longer and more demanding routes. There is 1 difficult trail available, and 31 moderate trails that can offer a good challenge.

Q: Can I find viewpoints with panoramic views along the trails?
A: Yes, Ares Del Maestrat is known for its stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e878827905" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mola d'Ares Trail (SL-CV 46)&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views from the flat-topped Mola d'Ares. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1104473" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Castle of Ares del Maestre&lt;/a&gt; provides expansive views over the Maestrat region.
tips: With a height of 1,320 meters above sea level, the Mola de Ares is a plateau generated by erosion and at the same time bordered by large cliffs, making it the highest point in the municipality of Ares. It is a place from which to enjoy the best panoramic views of the valleys and mountains of Els Ports and Alt Maestrat. A few years ago it was declared a Municipal Natural Area, given its great ecological, landscape, scientific and recreational value.

From these points you will have wonderful views towards the valleys and mountains of Els Ports and Alt Maestrat.

A few meters away you will find the ruins of the old castle, also an archaeological site since remains of the Iberian civilization and fragments of the Moorish walls have been found. All these elements and history made Ares del Maestre a military camp in the Middle Ages.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Mola d'Ares Trail (SL-CV 46)?
faqs.answer: The trail is approximately 6.6 kilometers (4.1 miles) long and typically takes around 1 hour and 55 minutes to complete. This duration is an estimate and can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this trail?
faqs.answer: The Mola d'Ares Trail (SL-CV 46) is classified as moderate. While some sections might be considered simple, you should be prepared for varying inclines, significant elevation changes, and potentially steep sections as it descends into ravines and valleys.

faqs.question: Is the Mola d'Ares Trail a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Mola d'Ares Trail (SL-CV 46) is a circular route, also known as the Camí de la Mola de Ares Circular, allowing you to return to your starting point without retracing your steps.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail is set around the picturesque village of Ares del Maestrat, which serves as a common starting or ending point. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's generally advisable to look for parking within or on the outskirts of Ares del Maestrat.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You can expect a diverse and constantly changing landscape. The route follows ancient bridle paths and features significant elevation changes, including descents into ravines and valleys. The defining characteristic is the 'mola' (flat-topped mountain) formation, offering broad panoramic views.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the Mola d'Ares Trail?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several notable features. These include the Geodetic Vertex of la Mola d'Ares, the Viewpoint of the Castle of Ares del Maestre, and the charming village of Ares del Maestrat itself. You'll also discover a series of historic flour mills with their intricate hydraulic systems, and the 16th-century Nevera dels Regatxols snow well.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed throughout much of the year. However, be aware that in winter, the area can be blanketed in snow, which adds a unique dimension to the scenery but may require appropriate gear. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for hiking.

faqs.question: Is the Mola d'Ares Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular trail are not detailed, generally, dogs are welcome on trails in natural areas in Spain, often requiring them to be on a leash. It's always best to check local signage or park regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the trail or the natural park?
faqs.answer: The Mola d'Ares Trail is located within the Paratge Natural Municipal La Mola d'Ares. Typically, there are no entrance fees or permits required for hiking on public trails within natural parks in this region. However, always respect local regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other hiking routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camí del la Mola d'Ares, Camí de Conquesta, and Comunitat Valenciana. Fredes - el Pinós, among others, offering connections to a wider network of paths in the area.

faqs.question: What makes the 'mola' geological formation unique?
faqs.answer: The 'mola' (or 'muela') is a distinctive geological formation characterized by its flat, plateau-like summit. The Mola d'Ares itself reaches an elevation of 1321 meters, with a prominent flat top spanning approximately 2 square kilometers, making it a unique feature of the landscape.

Viewpoint of the Castle of Ares del Maestre
